name: HGH (Somatropin)
alias: Human Growth Hormone / rHGH / Norditropin / Genotropin
mech: Endogenous Growth Hormone — GHR Full Agonist.
desc: Recombinant human growth hormone, identical to the 191-amino-acid pituitary peptide. Gold standard of the GH axis. Drives IGF-1 production in the liver, promotes lipolysis, protein synthesis, collagen production, and bone density. FDA-approved for GH deficiency, short stature, HIV wasting, and Turner syndrome. Widely used off-label for anti-aging, body composition, and injury recovery. Suppresses endogenous GH production during use. Subcutaneous administration preferred; IM increases peak GH but is not standard.
sides: Carpal tunnel, water retention, elevated blood glucose, acromegaly risk with high-dose chronic use, joint pain, potential IGF-1 driven proliferative cancer risk.
cancer: Theoretical (IGF-1 elevation — long-term supraphysiologic levels may promote tumor growth in hormone-sensitive cancers)
dev: Genentech / Novo Nordisk / Eli Lilly / Others
endo: YES — endogenous pituitary peptide
recon_vol: Follow manufacturer instructions
hormonal: Suppresses endogenous GH + HPG axis effects
trial: Extensive clinical trials spanning 40+ years. Well-established safety and efficacy profile.
purpose: GH Deficiency / Pediatric Growth / Body Composition / Longevity
subj: Water retention, joint pain, carpal tunnel, improved sleep, fat loss, muscle growth, skin elasticity

name: Ipamorelin
mech: Selective GH pulse — no cortisol/prolactin bleed. Most selective GHRP.
cycle: 8–12 wks on, 4 wks off. Low-risk continuous use acceptable.
sides: Mild: transient headache, facial flushing, tingling, minor hunger spike
endo: No (synthetic pentapeptide)
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC water → 250–500 mcg/0.1 mL
fasting: RECOMMENDED — inject fasted or 2h post-meal (food blunts GH pulse)
trial: Phase I/II completed. No Phase III. Widely referenced in longevity practice.
source: Compounding pharmacies (Rx, some regions); grey market
purpose: GH Pulse / Anti-Aging / Sleep / Recovery
subj: Warmth, deeper sleep, subtle muscle fullness over weeks

name: CJC-1295 (no DAC)
mech: Amplifies GH pulse amplitude. Preserves natural rhythm. Short-acting.
freq: 1–3× daily; always 15 min before GHRP partner
sides: Transient: flushing, tingling, headache, mild water retention
endo: Analog of endogenous GHRH
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC water → 100–200 mcg/0.05–0.1 mL
fasting: RECOMMENDED — inject fasted, 15 min before GHRP
trial: Derived from ConjuChem CJC-1293 research. Parent GHRH extensively studied.
source: Grey market; US compounding restricted (2024 FDA Cat. 2)
purpose: GH Pulse Amplification / Anti-Aging / Recomp
subj: Head rush at onset, deeper sleep, gradual fat loss / muscle density over weeks

name: CJC-1295 (with DAC)
mech: Sustained GH/IGF-1 via albumin binding (DAC). Blunts natural pulsatility. HL ~6–8 days.
sides: Water retention, appetite increase, potential pituitary desensitization, grogginess
cancer: Higher concern vs pulsatile — sustained IGF-1
endo: Modified GHRH analog
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC water → 1000 mcg/0.1–0.2 mL
trial: Limited human trials. Less preferred clinically than Mod GRF.
purpose: Sustained GH / TRT-style / Mass
stack: Not ideal to combine with GHRP long-term
subj: Continuous mild anabolic state, notable water retention/bloat

name: GHRP-6
mech: Strong GH secretagogue. Significant ghrelin mimicry → aggressive hunger.
sides: EXTREME hunger (major compliance issue), cortisol/prolactin elevation, water retention
endo: No
fasting: REQUIRED — fasting essential for maximal GH pulse
trial: Pioneer compound for GH secretagogue class. Multiple human studies 1980s–2000s.
purpose: GH Pulse / Appetite / Mass
subj: Ravenous hunger, strong GH flush. More sides than Ipamorelin.

name: GHRP-2
mech: Potent GH secretagogue. Less hunger than GHRP-6 but more cortisol/prolactin.
sides: Cortisol spike (significant), prolactin elevation, moderate hunger, water retention
endo: No
trial: Approved briefly in Japan for GHD diagnosis. Multiple Phase II trials.
purpose: GH Pulse / Strength / GHD Diagnosis
subj: Stronger GH flush than Ipamorelin, more sides

name: Sermorelin
mech: Native GHRH(1-29) fragment — physiologic GH pulse. Most natural GH peptide.
cycle: 3–6 months on, 1 month off; or TRT-continuous
sides: Mild flushing, headache, transient tingling, injection site
endo: Truncated form of endogenous GHRH(1-44)
fasting: RECOMMENDED — nightly injection, fasted before sleep
trial: FDA approved for pediatric GHD diagnosis. Extensive adult longevity clinical practice data.
source: Compounding pharmacies (Rx USA). Grey market also available.
purpose: Anti-Aging / GHD / Sleep / Gradual Recomp
subj: Subtle gradual — better sleep, body composition shift over months

name: Tesamorelin
mech: GHRH(1-44) analog. FDA-approved for HIV lipodystrophy visceral fat. GH/IGF-1 elevation.
sides: Water retention, joint pain, glucose changes, injection site reactions
endo: Modified analog of full-length GHRH(1-44)
recon_vol: 1–2 mL SW or BAC → 1 mg/mL
fasting: Not required (once daily, any time — pre-bed preferred)
trial: Landmark Phase III: TREAT, SAFARI, and extension studies. Primary visceral fat endpoint met.
source: Prescription (USA) — FDA approved. Compounding with Rx.
purpose: Visceral Fat Reduction / GH Elevation / Metabolic Health
subj: Progressive visceral fat loss over weeks/months, improved definition

name: IGF-1 LR3
mech: Direct IGF-1R agonist. Bypasses GH axis. Potent anabolic, anti-catabolic. HL 20–30h vs native IGF-1 (~20 min).
sides: Hypoglycemia (serious risk), organ growth, acromegalic features chronic use, nerve pain, carpal tunnel
cancer: HIGH — direct IGF-1R proliferative; contraindicated in cancer history
endo: Analog of endogenous IGF-1 — extended half-life version
recon_vol: 1% acetic acid (10–50 µL), then dilute with sterile PBS to 0.1–1 mg/mL
shelf_recon: 7–14 days @ 4°C in acetic acid/PBS; avoid freeze-thaw
trial: Extensively studied in growth deficiency. No approved human performance use.
purpose: Extreme Anabolism / Muscle Hyperplasia
subj: Rapid muscle fullness, possible hypoglycemia, 'swollen' sensation

name: MK-677 (Ibutamoren)
mech: Oral GHSR agonist. 24-hr sustained GH/IGF-1 elevation. Non-peptide small molecule.
sides: Water retention (significant), hunger, fatigue, insulin resistance, glucose elevation, prolactin raise, transient numbness
endo: No (non-peptide)
recon_vol: Oral capsule/liquid — no reconstitution needed
fasting: Not required (oral; take with or without food — some prefer fasted for sleep pulse)
hormonal: No HPG/HPA suppression. Prolactin moderately elevated.
phase: Phase IIb/III (hip fracture, Alzheimer's, HIV wasting)
trial: Extensive — multiple Phase II/III trials in elderly, HIV wasting. Studied for MCI/Alzheimer's.
purpose: GH + IGF-1 / Mass / Sleep / Anti-Aging
subj: Deep sleep, hunger, pumps, water weight. Less 'clean' than injectables.

name: Semaglutide
mech: GLP-1R agonism: CNS appetite suppression, gastric emptying delay, insulin sensitization. 7-day half-life via fatty acid chain.
sides: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation (first weeks), rare pancreatitis, gallbladder issues, muscle loss risk
cancer: Thyroid C-cell (rodent only — not confirmed human; boxed warning)
endo: Analog of endogenous GLP-1(7-37)
recon_vol: PRE-FILLED PEN (Ozempic/Wegovy). Research vials: 1–2 mL BAC → dose per protocol
shelf_recon: 28 days @ 2–8°C (reconstituted); Pen: 56 days after first use
trial: SUSTAIN (T2DM), STEP (obesity), SELECT (CV outcomes: −20% MACE). Best-in-class clinical evidence.
source: Prescription only. Compounding with Rx (USA). Grey market copycat rampant.
purpose: Weight Loss / T2DM / CV Risk Reduction
subj: Dramatic appetite suppression, 'food noise' elimination. Nausea first weeks.

name: Tirzepatide
mech: Dual GLP-1 + GIP receptor co-agonism. Superior fat loss vs GLP-1 alone. GIPR adds: energy expenditure, direct adipocyte effects.
sides: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea (less than semaglutide), pancreatitis risk, gallbladder, muscle loss, thyroid concern
cancer: Thyroid C-cell class warning (rodent — not confirmed human)
receptor: GLP-1R + GIPR (dual agonism — first-in-class dual incretin)
endo: Analog of GIP + GLP-1
recon_vol: PRE-FILLED PEN (commercial). Research vials: 1–2 mL BAC
shelf_recon: Pen: 21 days after first use @ 2–8°C or RT. Research vials: 28 days @ 2–8°C
trial: SURMOUNT: ~21% weight loss. SURPASS: superior HbA1c vs semaglutide. SURMOUNT-MMO CV trial ongoing.
source: Prescription only. Compounding. Significant grey market.
purpose: Weight Loss (superior) / T2DM / Metabolic Disease
subj: Superior to semaglutide: less nausea, stronger fat loss, appetite normalization

name: Retatrutide
mech: Triple GLP-1 + GIP + glucagon receptor agonism. Glucagon adds: energy expenditure increase, hepatic fat oxidation, additional lipolysis.
sides: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, tachycardia (glucagon effect), potential pancreatitis, muscle loss
receptor: GLP-1R + GIPR + Glucagon R (GCGR) — first-in-class TRIPLE agonist
endo: Analog of GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon
recon_vol: Research vials: 1–2 mL BAC → dose per protocol
trial: NEJM Phase 2: 24.2% weight loss (12mg, 48wks). TRIUMPH-4: 71.2 lb avg + OA benefit. Multiple ongoing: MASH, sleep apnea, CV, kidney.
source: Clinical trials only. Emerging grey market (2024–2025).
purpose: Weight Loss (best-in-class) / MASH / Metabolic Disease
subj: Anecdotal (grey): superior appetite suppression vs tirzepatide, rapid visceral fat loss

name: Cagrilintide
mech: Long-acting amylin analog. Slows gastric emptying, reduces appetite via different CNS pathway than GLP-1 agonists. Synergistic with semaglutide (CagriSema).
sides: GI events (less vomiting than GLP-1s alone), injection site reactions, mild nausea
endo: Analog of endogenous amylin (IAPP — islet amyloid polypeptide)
recon_vol: Research vials: 1–2 mL BAC → dose per protocol
phase: Phase III / NDA Filed (Dec 2025 — CagriSema combo)
approved: None yet — CagriSema NDA filed FDA Dec 2025
trial: REDEFINE 1 (NEJM 2025): CagriSema −20.4% weight loss, 60% achieved ≥20% loss. REDEFINE 2 (T2D): −13.7%. REIMAGINE 2: −14.2% + superior HbA1c.
purpose: Weight Loss (amylin mechanism) / Best as CagriSema combo
subj: Complementary to GLP-1: different CNS satiety pathway, additive fat loss, less vomiting than pure GLP-1

name: AOD-9604
mech: C-terminal hGH fragment. Stimulates lipolysis, inhibits lipogenesis — WITHOUT GH anabolic/growth effects.
sides: Very mild: occasional injection site reaction, headache. May gel if not properly reconstituted.
dev: Monash University / Prof. Frank Ng / Metabolic Pharmaceuticals
endo: Fragment of endogenous hGH C-terminus (177-191)
recon_vol: 1 mL 0.6% acetic acid + 2 mL BAC water (5:1 BAC:AA ratio). Gels in BAC-only — AA prevents gelation.
shelf_recon: 14–21 days @ 4°C. Pre-load syringes immediately to avoid gelation.
fasting: RECOMMENDED — fasted injection enhances lipolytic effect
trial: Metabolic Pharma Phase IIb: inconclusive at human doses studied. FDA GRAS for oral use only.
purpose: Fat Mobilization / Body Recomposition
stack: + GH peptide stack for additive lipolysis
subj: Subtle fat mobilization over weeks — modest vs GLP-1 class. Best evidence for visceral fat.

name: MOTS-c
mech: Mitochondria-derived peptide (mitokine). Activates AMPK → glucose uptake, fatty acid oxidation, 'exercise-like' metabolic effects.
sides: Minimal reported. Rare: mild fatigue, injection site reaction. Limited human data.
dev: Changhan Lee / USC Leonard Davis School
receptor: AMPK (metabolic master switch) / nuclear stress response
endo: YES — encoded by mitochondrial genome; declines with age
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 2.5–5 mg/mL
fasting: Not required; some prefer fasted (exercise mimetic — may enhance effect)
trial: Rodent: exercise mimetic, longevity, metabolic. First human proof-of-concept studies emerging.
purpose: Metabolic Enhancement / Fat Loss / Exercise Mimetic / Anti-Aging
subj: Anecdotal: improved insulin sensitivity, fat loss, exercise endurance. Comparable to exercise stimulus.

name: BPC-157
mech: Multi-target: angiogenesis, tendon/ligament fibroblast proliferation, gut mucosal healing, NO modulation, neuroprotection.
cycle: 4–12 weeks for injuries; gut: ongoing until resolved; 4–6 wks off
sides: Minimal. Theoretical: pro-angiogenic (tumor vascularity concern).
cancer: Theoretical — pro-angiogenic mechanism; caution in cancer history
dev: Prof. Predrag Sikirić — University of Zagreb
endo: Derived from human gastric juice protein BPC
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 250–500 mcg/0.1–0.2 mL (1 mg/mL = 0.25 mL per 250 mcg dose)
fasting: Not required; some inject near injury site (local)
trial: 500+ animal studies. Human topical trials (Bepecin). No large RCT in healthy humans yet.
source: Grey market globally. FDA Cat. 2 = US compounding banned (2024).
purpose: Tendon / Ligament / Joint / Gut Healing / Anti-Inflammatory
subj: Rapid pain reduction (3–7 days), accelerated injury healing, gut comfort improvement
notice: Acute pain: 3–7 days; injury healing: 2–6 weeks

name: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4)
mech: Actin sequestration → cell migration, angiogenesis, systemic wound healing, cardiac repair, neuroregeneration.
cycle: Loading: 2–4 weeks. Maintenance: 4–8 weeks. Off: 4–8 weeks.
sides: Fatigue, headache, transient nausea — generally well tolerated
endo: YES — found in virtually all nucleated cells; highest in platelets
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 2–4 mg/mL. Common: 2 mg vial + 1 mL BAC
trial: Human trials: cardiac repair Phase I/II, wound healing, multiple sclerosis research, stroke recovery.
source: Grey market. FDA Cat. 2 = US compounding banned (2024).
purpose: Systemic Healing / Muscle Recovery / CV Protection / Flexibility
subj: Systemic 'ease of movement', reduced inflammation, joint fluidity improvement

name: GHK-Cu
mech: Copper delivery → collagen synthesis, wound healing, anti-inflammation, neuroprotective gene expression, skin remodeling.
freq: Daily topical; once or twice daily SQ
sides: Very rare — copper toxicity at extreme doses only. Topical: rare mild irritation.
cancer: Low — research suggests potential anti-tumor properties
dev: Loren Pickart PhD — University of California
receptor: Cu²⁺-dependent enzymes / TGF-β1 / VEGF / FGF pathways
endo: YES — naturally in plasma, urine, saliva; declines with age
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 1–2 mg/mL. Topical: various preparations 0.05–10%
phase: Phase III (topical approved) / Phase II (injectable research)
approved: Topical approved (wound care) in multiple countries
trial: Extensive topical human trials (Iamin). Multiple skin/wound healing RCTs. Neuroprotection studies ongoing.
source: Topical: mainstream retail. Injectable SQ: compounding/grey market.
purpose: Collagen / Skin / Wound Healing / Anti-Aging / Neuroprotection
subj: Improved skin texture, wound closure speed, hair density improvement over months

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
mech: Master immune regulator — T-cell maturation, NK cell activation, dendritic cell function, cytokine modulation.
sides: Excellent safety. Rare: mild injection site reaction only.
cancer: None — adjunct in cancer immunotherapy research
dev: Allan Goldstein PhD — George Washington University
endo: YES — produced by thymic epithelial cells; declines sharply with age
recon_vol: 1–2 mL SW or BAC → 1.6 mg/mL (clinical dose)
trial: Extensive: HBV/HCV RCTs, sepsis, COVID-19, cancer immunotherapy, HIV. One of most clinically studied peptides.
source: Rx in many countries (Zadaxin). US compounding with Rx. Grey market available.
grey: Yes — but legitimate Rx in many countries
purpose: Immune Regulation / Anti-Aging / Infection / Cancer Adjunct
subj: Subtle resilience to illness, improved immune/vaccine response, reduced sick days over months

name: KPV
mech: Anti-inflammatory — blocks NF-κB pathway. Derived from α-MSH, potent gut anti-inflammatory.
cycle: 4–8 weeks for gut; ongoing for IBD management
sides: Minimal — rare transient nausea
endo: Derived from endogenous α-MSH C-terminus
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 500 mcg–1 mg/mL
fasting: Not required; oral often used for gut-specific delivery
trial: Mostly animal data. IBD context promising. Limited human trials.
purpose: Gut Healing / Anti-Inflammatory / IBD / Wound Repair
subj: Reduced gut pain, calmer digestion, improved IBD symptoms

name: SS-31 (Elamipretide)
mech: Stabilizes cardiolipin on IMM → restores electron transport chain → reduces ROS → mitochondrial protection.
sides: Injection site reactions (mild), transient fatigue — very well tolerated
dev: Hazel Szeto / Cornell University / Stealth BioTherapeutics
endo: No (synthetic D-amino acid tetrapeptide)
recon_vol: 1–2 mL SW or BAC → 0.5–4 mg/mL
phase: Phase III (HFpEF, FSGS kidney, primary mitochondrial myopathy, Barth syndrome)
trial: Multiple Phase II/III: heart failure, FSGS kidney disease, mitochondrial myopathy. Strong human safety data.
purpose: Mitochondrial Health / Heart / Kidney / Anti-Aging Energy
subj: Improved exercise capacity, energy, reduced fatigue over weeks — notable in mitochondrial dysfunction

name: Semax
mech: BDNF + NGF upregulation, neuroprotection, cognitive enhancement, anti-anxiety, stroke protection.
cycle: 2–4 weeks on, 1–2 weeks off (nootropic); longer for stroke recovery
sides: Mild headache, irritability (rare), vivid dreams, occasional anxiety at high doses
dev: Institute of Molecular Genetics / Russian Academy of Sciences
endo: Analog of ACTH(4-7) — derived from endogenous corticotropin
recon_vol: Nasal drops: pre-formulated 0.1–1% solution. SQ: BAC, 1–2 mL → 200–600 mcg/dose
shelf_recon: Pre-formulated nasal: use per label. SQ: 28 days @ 2–8°C
trial: Extensive Russian clinical trials: stroke recovery, ADHD, cognitive enhancement. Approved for ischemic stroke.
purpose: Cognitive Enhancement / BDNF / Neuroprotection / Focus / Mood
subj: Sharp mental clarity, mood lift, 'clean stimulation without jitteriness'

name: Selank
mech: Anxiolytic, anti-stress, immune modulation, cognitive stabilization. No dependency, no withdrawal, no sedation.
cycle: 2–4 weeks on, 1–2 weeks off; or as needed
sides: Very rare — mild transient sedation, vivid dreams at high dose. Excellent profile.
receptor: GABA-A modulation / serotonin uptake / BDNF/TrkB / IL-6
endo: Analog of Tuftsin — endogenous immunopeptide from IgG
recon_vol: Nasal drops: pre-formulated. SQ: BAC, 1–2 mL → 250–500 mcg/dose
trial: Multiple Russian RCTs: anxiety, depression. Approved for generalized anxiety.
purpose: Anti-Anxiety / Cognitive Enhancement / Stress / Immune
subj: Calm focused state, reduced overthinking, 'anxiolytic without sedation'

name: Dihexa
mech: Synaptogenesis via HGF/MET. Claimed millions× more potent than BDNF in oligomers (rodent data). Dendritic spine growth.
sides: Unknown. Theoretical: synaptogenic over-stimulation, MET oncogenic signaling.
cancer: ELEVATED — MET is proto-oncogene (driver in many tumor types)
dev: Joseph Harding / Jay Wright — Washington State University
receptor: HGF/MET receptor system (Hepatocyte Growth Factor receptor)
endo: No (synthetic angiotensin IV analog)
recon_vol: Oral/topical cream — lipophilic, no standard reconstitution. Some use DMSO carrier for topical.
trial: Animal cognitive enhancement only. No human trials.
purpose: Extreme Cognitive Enhancement / Neuroplasticity / Memory
subj: Anecdotal: enhanced recall, processing speed, pattern recognition. Highly variable. Effects persist weeks.

name: Cerebrolysin
mech: Complex porcine brain peptide mix. BDNF/NGF-like, reduces tau phosphorylation, anti-apoptotic, synaptic plasticity.
sides: Rare: mild allergic reaction, injection site pain, mild fever, nausea. Very well tolerated.
endo: Porcine brain hydrolysate — mix of endogenous-like neuropeptides
recon_vol: Pre-filled ampoules (5–30 mL). Dilute in 0.9% NaCl for IV infusion. No powder reconstitution.
trial: Extensive: Cochrane reviews on stroke, TBI, Alzheimer's benefit. Multiple large RCTs in Europe/Asia.
source: Pharmacy in 40+ countries (Rx). Not FDA approved USA — grey market available.
purpose: Neuroprotection / Stroke Recovery / TBI / Alzheimer's / Cognitive Enhancement
subj: Post-injury: profound neurological restoration. Healthy: brain fog clearing, sharper cognition

name: Epithalon
mech: Telomerase activation (lengthens telomeres), melatonin normalization, epigenetic anti-aging gene modulation, antioxidant.
cycle: 10–20 day course, 2×/year; OR 5–7 day monthly course
sides: Excellent safety. Rare: mild injection site reaction. Decades of Russian use.
cancer: Potentially protective — may suppress tumor growth (animal studies)
dev: Prof. Vladimir Khavinson — St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation
receptor: Pineal epigenetic targets / Telomere regulation / PCNA
endo: Derived from endogenous pineal gland peptide (Epithalamin)
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 5–10 mg/mL
fasting: Not required; evening injection preferred (circadian alignment)
trial: Russian clinical research: telomerase activation in human fibroblasts. 12% lifespan extension in SHR mice. Multiple Khavinson Institute studies.
source: Grey market — widely used in longevity circles
purpose: Anti-Aging / Telomere Protection / Longevity / Sleep / Circadian
subj: Subtle cumulative — better sleep, gradual vitality. Effects accumulate over cycles.

name: FOXO4-DRI
mech: Senolytic: disrupts FOXO4-p53 binding in senescent 'zombie' cells → releases p53 → selective apoptosis of senescent cells only.
cycle: 6-day course (2 mg EOD × 3); repeat 1–3×/year ONLY. NOT chronic daily use.
sides: Mild: injection site, fatigue, nausea, muscle soreness. Long-term risks unknown.
cancer: Theoretical concern — MET-adjacent pathways (monitor in cancer history)
receptor: p53 (disrupts FOXO4-p53 nuclear interaction in senescent cells)
endo: Mimics endogenous FOXO4 protein domain (D-amino acid — protease resistant)
recon_vol: Dissolve in 0.1% acetic acid first, then dilute with sterile PBS to desired concentration (0.5–5 mg/mL)
shelf_recon: 7–14 days @ 4°C in PBS; D-AA highly stable
trial: Landmark Nature Medicine 2017 (de Keizer): mice — improved fur, kidney, fitness. Cartilage regeneration studies. No completed human RCTs.
purpose: Senolysis / Zombie Cell Clearance / Tissue Rejuvenation / Longevity
subj: Anecdotal: reduced chronic pain, improved energy 2–4 weeks post-course. High variability.

name: Humanin
mech: Mitochondria-derived peptide (mitokine). Cytoprotective, neuroprotective (Alzheimer's), anti-inflammatory. Endogenous longevity factor.
sides: Unknown. Animal studies suggest excellent safety.
cancer: Unknown — potentially protective vs apoptosis in normal cells
dev: Ikuo Nishimoto — Keio University School of Medicine
receptor: FPRL1 (FPR2) / IL-6 receptor complex / IGFBP-3 / gp130
endo: YES — encoded in mitochondrial 16S rRNA; declines significantly with age
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 2–5 mg/mL
trial: Rodent: lifespan extension, Alzheimer's prevention, CV protection. Human proof-of-concept studies 2022–2024.
purpose: Mitochondrial Cytoprotection / Neuroprotection / Anti-Aging / Longevity
subj: Very anecdotal. Possible energy improvement. Neuroprotection effects unclear short-term.

name: Epithalon + Thymalin Protocol
mech: Combined thymic + pineal bioregulators. Thymalin: T-cell immune restoration. Epithalon: telomere + melatonin + longevity epigenetics.
dose: Thymalin: 10 mg/day IM + Epithalon: 5–10 mg/day SQ
sides: Very rare. Excellent long-term safety in Russian cohort studies.
receptor: Thymic T-lymphocyte receptors + Pineal epigenetic targets
endo: Both derived from endogenous thymus and pineal gland peptides
recon_vol: Separate vials. Each: 1–2 mL BAC. 10 mg/day Thymalin IM + 5–10 mg/day Epithalon SQ
trial: Khavinson 40-year follow-up: reduced all-cause mortality ~2×, cancer incidence reduction in treated elderly cohort.
purpose: Full Longevity Protocol / Immune + Telomere + Epigenetic Anti-Aging
stack: Core longevity stack — add GHK-Cu and Semax for comprehensive protocol
subj: Cumulative long-term benefit — immune resilience, reduced illness, gradual vitality improvement

name: Pinealon
mech: Brain bioregulator — epigenetically activates neuronal genes suppressed by aging. Penetrates BBB. Retinal protection.
sides: Very rare. Minimal adverse events in Russian research.
receptor: Pineal / brain epigenetic targets / histone modulation
endo: Derived from endogenous pineal brain peptide
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 1–5 mg/mL
trial: Khavinson research: cognitive preservation elderly, retinal protection. Limited independent replication.
purpose: Neuro Anti-Aging / Brain Longevity / Retinal / Cognitive Preservation
subj: Subtle cumulative — improved mental acuity, memory preservation. Hard to assess acutely.

name: PT-141 (Bremelanotide)
mech: Central sexual arousal via melanocortin system. Works in CNS (not vascular like PDE5i). Effective in both sexes.
sides: Nausea (40% users), flushing, vomiting (dose-dependent), BP increase, hyperpigmentation (minimal)
cancer: None (MC3/4 expression — no known proliferative risk)
endo: Metabolite of Melanotan-II / derivative of α-MSH
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 1 mg/mL (standard). Clinical pen: pre-filled.
trial: FDA approved 2019. Phase III: BMT-301, BMT-305. Broad off-label use in men.
source: Rx (FDA approved). Compounding. Significant grey market.
purpose: Sexual Arousal / Libido / Erectile Function / HSDD
subj: Onset 30–60 min: sexual desire, physical arousal response. Nausea management critical.

name: Melanotan-II
mech: Pan-melanocortin agonist — tanning (MC1R), sexual arousal (MC4R CNS), appetite suppression, spontaneous erections.
sides: Nausea/vomiting (significant), flushing, spontaneous erections (involuntary), mole darkening, new nevi formation (MONITOR!), BP changes
cancer: SIGNIFICANT concern — MC1R melanocyte activation; documented dysplastic nevi/melanoma reports (causality debated but serious)
dev: Mac Hadley / Victor Hruby — University of Arizona
endo: Synthetic analog of α-MSH
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 0.5–1 mg/mL. Low dose first (0.25 mg) to assess nausea.
fasting: Not required; some report less nausea with food
trial: UA Phase I/II: tanning/erection confirmed. Development halted. PT-141 derived as safer alternative.
grey: Popular but risky — grey market only
purpose: Tanning / Sexual Function / Appetite Suppression
stack: Standalone (do not combine with other melanocortins)
subj: Rapid tanning, strong libido, spontaneous erections. Nausea dominant first doses.

name: Oxytocin
mech: Neuropeptide: social bonding, trust, anxiety reduction, uterine contraction. Also: anti-inflammatory, gut healing, immune.
cycle: Chronic possible intranasal; use as needed or 8–12 week trials
sides: IV: uterine hyperstimulation risk, hyponatremia (large doses). Intranasal: rare headache, possible paradoxical anxiety.
dev: Vincent du Vigneaud — Cornell University (Nobel Prize 1955)
receptor: Oxytocin receptor (OXTR) — brain, uterus, heart, gut, T-cells
endo: YES — produced in hypothalamus, released from posterior pituitary
recon_vol: Nasal: pre-formulated 40 IU/mL (10 IU/spray). SQ: BAC, 1–2 mL
shelf_recon: Pre-formulated nasal: per label. SQ: 14–21 days @ 2–8°C
trial: FDA approved 1950s (obstetric). Intranasal research: autism, PTSD, social anxiety, addiction — mixed results.
source: Rx (obstetric). Compounding pharmacies. Grey market nasal spray widely available.
purpose: Social Bonding / Anti-Anxiety / PTSD / Gut Healing / Cardiac
subj: Warmth, social ease, empathy, reduced anxiety. Paradoxical effects possible in some.

name: Gonadorelin
mech: Pulsatile GnRH → LH + FSH release → testosterone production + spermatogenesis. Used to maintain testicular function during TRT/SARM.
cycle: Concurrent with TRT/SARM use — ongoing as needed
sides: Injection site reaction, rare headache, flushing. Excellent safety.
endo: YES — endogenous GnRH from hypothalamus
recon_vol: 1–2 mL BAC → 100 mcg/0.1 mL (1 mg/mL stock)
hormonal: Maintains LH/FSH/testosterone production (therapeutic goal). Continuous use = OPPOSITE effect (desensitization).
desens: HIGH if given CONTINUOUSLY (must pulse 2–3×/week only)
trial: FDA approved for hypogonadism, cryptorchidism, fertility. Widely used as TRT/SARM adjunct.
purpose: LH/FSH Stimulation / Testicular Function / Fertility Preservation
stack: + TRT / SARMs (as adjunct to preserve HPG axis)
subj: Maintains testicular volume, testosterone production, fertility during hormonal therapies

name: VIP (Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide)
mech: Anti-inflammatory neuropeptide: bronchodilation, vasodilation, gut motility, cytokine storm reduction, neuroprotection, circadian regulation.
sides: IV: hypotension, flushing, nausea. Intranasal: well tolerated.
cancer: Theoretical (VPAC in some tumors) — generally anti-inflammatory protective
dev: Sami Said — Albert Einstein College of Medicine
receptor: VPAC1, VPAC2 receptors — widely expressed (lung, gut, brain, immune)
endo: YES — produced in neurons, gut, lungs, immune cells
recon_vol: For infusion: dilute in 0.9% NaCl. Intranasal: BAC, 1–2 mL → 25–100 mcg/dose
trial: Aviptadil Phase III (COVID-19 ARDS): significant mortality reduction. Long COVID research ongoing.
source: Compounding pharmacies. Grey market growing (post-COVID interest).
purpose: Anti-Inflammatory / Lung / Gut / Long COVID / Cognitive / Circadian
subj: Intranasal: calm, anti-anxiety, improved sleep, cognitive clarity, reduced inflammation

name: DSIP
mech: Sleep induction, stress reduction, GH release modulation, LH/CRH regulation, antioxidant.
sides: Very rare — mild headache, vivid dreams. Excellent safety.
dev: Monnier et al. — University of Basel
receptor: DSIP receptor (putative — not fully characterized) / GHRH axis
endo: YES — found naturally in brain, hypothalamus, pituitary
fasting: Not required; inject ~30 min before sleep
trial: Soviet/European research: sleep quality, stress resilience. Limited modern RCTs.
purpose: Sleep Quality / Stress Resilience / Mild GH Support
subj: Deeper sleep, more restful nights, reduced stress response
